zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Fisher於1527年出生在英國英格蘭貝德福爾亨洛福時，她的父親John Fisher47歲，母親Anna45歲。她於1538年1月30日與Sir John Richard Marsh Sr.在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1570年11月28日在英國英格蘭米德萊可特查阿培爾享年43歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可可拉肯維爾 聖 詹姆斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Fisher于1527年出生在英国英格兰貝德福爾亨洛福时，她的父亲John Fisher47岁，母亲Anna45岁。她于1538年1月30日与Sir John Richard Marsh Sr.在英国英格兰斯塔福爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1570年11月28日在英国英格兰米德萊可特查阿培爾享年43岁，埋葬在英国米德萊可可拉肯維爾 聖 詹姆斯。

nameMeanings.name: Fisher
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a fisherman, from Middle English fis(sc)her(e) ‘fisherman’ (Old English fiscere). In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from many other languages, including German Fischer and its Slavic(ized) variant Fišer (see Fiser ), Dutch Visser , Hungarian Halász (see Halasz ), Italian Pescatore , Slovenian Ribič (see Ribic ), and Croatian Ribić or Ribar .

English: in a few cases, possibly a topographic name for someone who lived near a fish weir on a river, from Middle English fis(sc)hwere, fisshyar ‘fish weir’ (Old English fiscwer, fiscgear), or a habitational name from a place so named, such as Fisher in North Mundham, Sussex.
Irish: translation into English of Gaelic Ó Bradáin ‘descendant of Bradán’, a personal name meaning ‘salmon’. See Braden .

variants.name: Fischer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Otto, Erwin, Gerhard, Helmut, Fritz, Klaus, Horst, Dieter, Franz, Ernst.

German, Danish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a fisherman, from Middle High German visch, German Fisch ‘fish’ + the agent suffix -er. This form of the surname is also established in some other parts of Europe, notably in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, and the Netherlands. In Poland, Czechia, Croatia, and Slovenia it is (also) established in the Slavicized forms Fiszer (Polish) and Fišer (see Fiser ). Compare Fisher .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Fisher was born in 1527, in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Fisher, was 47 and her mother, Anna, was 45. She married Sir John Richard Marsh Sr. on 30 January 1538, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 28 November 1570, in Whitechapel, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 43, and was buried in Clerkenwell St James, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Fisher est née en 1527, à Henlow, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Fisher, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Anna, avait 45 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Richard Marsh Sr. le 30 janvier 1538, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 28 novembre 1570, à Whitechapel, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterrée à Clerkenwell St James, Middlesex, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Fisherが1527年にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアヘンロウで生まれた時，父John Fisherは47歳，母Annaは45歳であった。1538年1月30日にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアでSir John Richard Marsh Sr.と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1570年11月28日にイギリスイギリスミッドレセックスウィテチャペルで43歳で死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスクラーケンウエル スト ジャムスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Fisher(이)가 1527년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 헨로에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Fisher(은)는 47세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anna(은)는 45세였다. 그녀는 1538년 1월 30일 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 Sir John Richard Marsh Sr.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1570년 11월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 휘테차플에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 클레르켄웰 스트 자메스에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Sir John Richard Marsh Sr. em 30 de janeiro de 1538, em Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Fisher родилась в 1527 г. в местности хенлоу, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John Fisher было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Anna, 45. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Richard Marsh Sr. 30 января 1538 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 28 ноября 1570 г. в местности уитечапел, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 43 лет и была похоронена в местности клиркенвелл ст джемесь, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Fisher nació en 1527, en Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Fisher, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Anna, tenía 45 años. Se casó con Sir John Richard Marsh Sr. el 30 de enero de 1538, en Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 28 de noviembre de 1570, en Whitechapel, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultada en Clerkenwell St James, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
